channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How a Minnesota limo driver invented the tech that’s changing college football
item.description: College football is suddenly in a constant state of change. The transfer portal, conference realignment, players making millions in NIL deals, the first-ever 12-team playoff—if you cryogenically froze someone five years ago and woke them up today, they wouldn’t recognize the sport.
item.dc:creator: Paul Mueller, Fast Company

item.title: 6 new tools to upgrade your home office
item.description: The home office is under fire as 2025 gets underway. CEOs, politicians, and one particular billionaire have been increasingly vocal in their opposition to employees working from home.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: FireScam Malware: How Fake Apps Steal Your Personal Data
item.description: Fake apps are spreading malware that steals everything on your phone, but you can protect yourself with these simple steps.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Meloni says Italy is exploring deals on telecoms security, but denies private talks with Musk
item.description: Italy’s Premier Giorgia Meloni says her government is in talks with private companies, including Elon Musk’s SpaceX, over the country’s telecoms security system, but denied having discussed the issue privately with Musk.
item.dc:creator: Giada Zampano, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: AMD and Nvidia could be set for epic GPU showdown with RX 9070 and 9070 XT going on sale at the same time as the RTX 5080
item.description: AMD RX 9070 and 9070 XT graphics cards might be here sooner than you think – pre-orders could go live on January 23.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Google faces privacy lawsuit as judge highlights data governance concerns
item.description: A federal judge in San Francisco has ruled that a privacy lawsuit against Google, alleging the company improperly collected personal data from mobile devices, can proceed.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta tries letting Facebook Marketplace users view eBay listings as way to resolve EU charges
item.description: Meta said Wednesday that it will allow some Facebook users to view eBay listings on its Marketplace service, as it tries out a possible way to resolve European Union charges of anticompetitive behavior that the bloc leveled last year.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 9 buoni propositi IT per il 2025
item.description: I CIO sono molto ambiziosi. Per l’anno appena iniziato, non sembrano volersi accontentare dello status quo e, al contrario, fissano grandi obiettivi, secondo innumerevoli sondaggi realizzati di recente sui dirigenti IT.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Meta tests letting Facebook Marketplace users view eBay listings in a bid to appease the EU
item.description: Meta is testing a feature that will let Facebook users browse eBay listings on Facebook Marketplace before being redirected to complete the purchase on eBay. The test, which is being launched to a small group in Germany, France, and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Meta rolls back hate speech rules as Zuckerberg cites 'recent elections' as a catalyst
item.description: It wasn't just fact-checking that Meta scrapped from its platforms as it prepares for the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Japan links this Chinese hacking group to cyberattacks on its national security
item.description: Japan on Wednesday linked more than 200 cyberattacks over the past five years targeting the country’s national security and high technology data to a Chinese hacking group, MirrorFace, detailing their tactics and calling on government agencies and businesses to reinforce preventive measures.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Automakers Showcase Latest Advances at CES 2024
item.description: Latest news on automakers' innovations, electric vehicles, and sustainable energy solutions showcased at CES 2024 in Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal News Network’s AI & Data Exchange 2025
item.description: AI in action: How agencies are building out trusted artificial intelligence capabilities The post Federal News Network’s AI & Data Exchange 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Closing the digital gap: The importance of secure development practices in government IT modernization
item.description: In today's fast-paced digital landscape, effective government IT modernization is crucial for agencies to achieve their missions. The post Closing the digital gap: The importance of secure development practices in government IT modernization first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: 7+ speedy steps to free up space on your Mac
item.description: Computers get clogged with digital “stuff” over time, and while we all like to think we’re good at managing all that D-detritus, there’s somehow never quite enough time to clean things up.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft is finally fighting back against the rise of SteamOS by overhauling Windows 11 to be better on handhelds
item.description: Windows 11 is often cited as the worst thing about handheld gaming PCs - but it looks like Microsoft could soon fix this.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Sam Altman’s sister accuses OpenAI CEO of sexually abusing her
item.description: She accused the OpenAI chief executive officer of sexually abusing her for almost a decade, in a lawsuit filed in federal court.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Biden: Biggest regret failing to counter misinformation, including from Trump
item.description: President Biden in an interview published Wednesday reflected on the highs and lows of his administration, stating his biggest regret was failing to challenge misinformation — including claims touted by President-elect Trump about the New Year's Day attacks.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: New research shows a quarter of freshwater animals are threatened with extinction
item.description: New research shows about a quarter of animals living in rivers, lakes and other freshwater sources are threatened with extinction.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: ByteDance's Lemon8 gains traction amid TikTok ban threat as creators push the app
item.description: Hearing a lot about Lemon8 lately? You’re not the only one. Amid a looming U.S. ban on TikTok, content creators have been pushing the platform's sister app.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Nvidia’s Project DIGITS puts AI supercomputing chips on the desktop
item.description: Nvidia has built its generative AI (genAI) business on delivering massive computing capacity to data centers where it can be used to train and refine large language models (LLMs).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Fubo combining with Disney's Hulu + Live TV; lawsuit against Venu Sports settled
item.description: Disney's Hulu + Live TV and sports streaming service Fubo are combining in a deal that will also see the settlement of a lawsuit against the creation of Venu Sports.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Planes, trains and third-party risks
item.description: Christmas Eve (and Christmas Day) are arguably the most important time-frame for transportation companies. So it was a big deal when an American Airlines system glitch forced the airline to ask the government for a full shutdown on Christmas Eve.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: It is easier than ever to disable Location Sharing on your Android phone
item.description: Google Maps Location Sharing is now built into Android settings; no need for Maps settings. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses the welcome improvement for Android users.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2025 Must Turn AI Possibilities into Realities
item.description: Cloud companies need to move beyond "primitives" and provide developers with solutions, not just AI building blocks, in order to turn AI possibilities into realities.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Treasury hack: Lawmakers seek more details on scope, third-party vulnerabilities
item.description: The Treasury hack is sparking new questions about how agencies ensure the security of third-party technology service providers. The post Treasury hack: Lawmakers seek more details on scope, third-party vulnerabilities first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took police more than a week to publicly identify Debrina Kawam, 57, as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train last month. But on the internet, it took just hours for a false name to begin spreading.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AI PCs
item.description: Virtually every aspect of technology has been affected, and potentially improved, by artificial intelligence (AI). PCs are no exception. While the meaning of “AI PC” is still evolving, it generally refers to a computer specifically designed to excel at AI-powered tasks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
